metadata.notes: NL injury tracker. Community-maintained MLB injury database tracking NL player injuries, timelines, and return statuses. Contributions welcome via PR.

injuries.details: Tore right ACL before 2021 All-Star break. Surgery and missed rest of 2021 and most of 2022.
Tore left ACL in Pittsburgh game. Surgery and rehab through 2024-2025. Two surgically repaired knees.
Not ready for Opening Day. First game back May 23 vs Padres — homered on first pitch after 362 days.
Late scratch from weightlifting. Hit leadoff HR next day. Cleared for All-Star Game.
Grade 1 strain vs Rockies at Coors Field. 10-day IL. Reinstated Aug 15.
Brief IL stint in late Aug after hamstring recovery.
7-game hitless stretch Sept. Moved from leadoff to 6th/7th by Snitker. Manager friction. Yankees trade interest reported.
Mystery stomach bug, no diagnosis after extensive testing. Lost ~20-25 lbs (175 to ~150) in two weeks. Missed Opening Day and Japan series.
Diminished strength, reduced bat speed, mechanical swing flaws. Battled shortstop transition. Career-low .258 AVG, .732 OPS. Declared 'season over' early Aug.
Went through anger then sadness. Reframed mindset in August to accept failure and focus on small contributions.
Bouts of lower back discomfort managed day-to-day throughout season.
Career-worst regular season (.258 AVG) then .317 over final 47 games. 6 hits in Wild Card sweep vs Reds. Dodgers won 2025 World Series — Betts had clutch 2-run single Game 6 to force Game 7.
Sprained right ankle in final game of 2024 regular season. Played through pain in postseason including World Series. Missed 2 NCLS games.
Debridement + loose body removal. Ligaments healed enough to avoid reconstruction. 8 weeks post-procedure could hit for first time. Could not run until late Feb 2025.
Flared during spring training at Camelback Ranch. Sidelined for both Tokyo Series games as precaution.
Aggravated ankle slipping in shower at home. 10-day IL (April 3-11). Returned and felt best since injury.

Career-best .376/1.078 OPS pace through 30 games, then worst power slump (41-game HR drought May 11 - June 28). Batting avg fell from .374 to .297, OPS from 1.078 to .840 over 49 games. Daily 1.5-hr ankle treatment.
Late scratch vs Reds with stinger in neck/shoulder (recurred issue). Not considered serious; returned within 2 days.

Hit .250 Wild Card, .200 NLDS. NLCS Game 1 walk-off HR. Walk-off HR in World Series Game 3 — second career WS walk-off (only player with multiple). Dodgers won 2025 WS. Season: .295/.367/.502, 147G, 24HR, 90RBI, 139 wRC+, 3.9 fWAR.
